You run $3\frac{1}{4}$ miles as a part of an exercise routine. You run $\frac{2}{5}$ of that distance on a hill. You run down a slope for the last $\frac{1}{2}$ of the hill. On how many miles of your run do you run down the slope? Write your answer as a fraction or mixed number in simplest form.

\underline{\hspace{1cm}} mile(s)
